title: Air-Fryer Negima Yakitori
summary: Sticky, juicy chicken and spring onion skewers inspired by Japanese Negima Yakitori – but made easy in the air fryer. Served with rice, edamame and Padrón peppers, it’s the kind of lunch that makes everyone else jealous.

method.description: Cut your spring onions and chicken thighs into roughly 2cm-sized pieces.
Thread the chicken and spring onion pieces tightly onto skewers, alternating between them. Season with salt and white pepper and set aside.
In a small saucepan, combine the soy sauce, brown sugar, sesame oil, and mirin. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat, then reduce to a simmer and cook for around 10 minutes until slightly thickened.
Toss the Padrón peppers with olive oil and salt.
Preheat the air fryer to 200°C. Add the skewers, brush with some glaze, and drizzle with a little olive oil. Cook for 9-10 minutes, brushing with more glaze halfway through.
Add the Padron peppers to the air fryer, brush the chicken again with glaze, and cook for another 5-6 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and the peppers are charred.
Cook the rice according to package instructions, adding the edamame 5 minutes before the end of the cooking time.

Spoon the edamame rice into your lunch boxes, then top with the skewers and Padrón peppers. Dust with shichimi togarashi, if using.
Leave to stand for a bit and once cooled, box up and stash in the fridge until you need them - they'll keep for up to 3 days.

tips: Store leftovers in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 3 days.
Reheat gently in the microwave or air fryer before serving.
If you don't have Shichimi Togarashi, use sesame seeds and chilli flakes as a substitute.
Use honey instead of brown sugar for a slightly different sweetness.
Replace padrón peppers with green bell peppers or shishito peppers.
Swap chicken thighs for chicken breast if preferred.

chefs.name: Jodie Nixon
chefs.summary: Jodie is a recipe writer, Mob food producer, and professional snacker. When she's not recreating all your childhood classics, she's creating delicious veg-forward dishes that'll prove you don't always need meat to have a good time. She's one hell of a talented cook.
